dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Another Sunday. Another family dinner. And another interrogation.

dialogue.speaker: Jia's Mother
dialogue.line: Jia-ya, look at your cousin, Sunmi. She's already planning her second child. And you? Still single at twenty-nine!

dialogue.speaker: Jia's Aunt
dialogue.line: It's not that she's not pretty, Jeong-ah. She just needs to lower her standards a little. Or maybe try harder?

dialogue.speaker: Han Jia
dialogue.line: Eommoni, Ajumma. I’m doing perfectly fine. My career is thriving. My new project just got approved.

dialogue.speaker: Jia's Mother
dialogue.line: Career, career, career! What's a career if you're going to grow old alone? Who will take care of you?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Alone. That word always stings. Not because I'm afraid of it, but because they make it sound like a disease.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My company just promoted me to team lead. I'm finally seeing the fruits of my labor. Why can't they see that's enough?

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: SIGH

dialogue.speaker: Han Jia
dialogue.line: I'm not 'alone.' I have friends, colleagues, a full life. Marriage isn't the only path to happiness.

dialogue.speaker: Jia's Mother
dialogue.line: Oh, for heaven's sake, Jia! Just go on a few blind dates. Meet someone nice. You're not getting any younger!

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Blind dates. The endless parade of awkward conversations, forced smiles, and men who just want a trophy wife. No, thank you.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: But... what if there was another way? A way to shut them all up, to buy myself some peace and quiet, without actually sacrificing my independence?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: The thought, absurd and desperate, flickered through my mind: A contract marriage.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: It was a ridiculous idea, something out of a drama. Who would even agree to such a thing?

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Then, a face popped into my head. Someone equally plagued by family expectations. Someone I'd known since we were kids.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Lee Mincheol. The quiet, reliable son of the Lee Group. Also twenty-nine. Also single. Also, probably, just as fed up as I was.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: He was always so calm, so logical. The perfect business partner, even for something as outlandish as this.

dialogue.speaker: SFX
dialogue.line: RING! RING!

dialogue.speaker: Han Jia
dialogue.line: Oh, excuse me. I need to take this.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: I grabbed my phone, eager for an escape. The caller ID made my breath hitch.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Lee Mincheol. Speak of the devil.

dialogue.speaker: Han Jia
dialogue.line: Hello?

dialogue.speaker: Lee Mincheol
dialogue.line: Jia. It's Mincheol. Do you have a moment to talk? There's something important I need to discuss with you.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: His voice was as calm and steady as ever, but there was an unusual edge to it. A hint of urgency.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Could it be? Was he thinking what I was thinking?

dialogue.speaker: Han Jia
dialogue.line: Mincheol? What's up? Is everything okay?

dialogue.speaker: Lee Mincheol
dialogue.line: I'm fine. But... I have a proposition for you. A rather unconventional one.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: My heart started pounding. This was either going to be the most insane conversation of my life, or the most brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: NARRATOR
dialogue.line: Maybe... just maybe... we were meant to be partners in crime.

summary: Jia endures another family dinner filled with nagging about her single status. Frustrated and desperate for a solution to escape the pressure, she contemplates a contract marriage. Just as the idea forms, her childhood friend, Lee Mincheol, calls with an 'unconventional proposition.'
